# NepDate - Complete API Reference

Version: 2.0.7 | License: MIT | NuGet: https://www.nuget.org/packages/NepDate/ | GitHub: https://github.com/RajuPrasai/NepDate

---

## Getting Started

**Install:**
```
dotnet add package NepDate
```

**Quick start:**
```csharp
using NepDate;

// Today in BS
var today = NepaliDate.Now;
Console.WriteLine(today); // e.g. "2083-02-19"

// BS to AD
var bs = new NepaliDate(2080, 6, 15);
DateTime ad = bs.EnglishDate;

// AD to BS
var bs2 = new NepaliDate(DateTime.Today);
```

---

## NepaliDate struct

`namespace NepDate`
`readonly partial struct NepaliDate : IComparable<NepaliDate>, IEquatable<NepaliDate>, IFormattable`
(net8.0 also: `ISpanFormattable, IParsable<NepaliDate>`)

### Constructors and factory methods

```csharp
// From BS year, month, day
NepaliDate(int year, int month, int day)

// From DateTime (AD to BS)
NepaliDate(DateTime englishDate)

// Today in BS
static NepaliDate NepaliDate.Now { get; }

// Parse from string
static NepaliDate NepaliDate.Parse(string s)

// Non-throwing parse
static bool NepaliDate.TryParse(string s, out NepaliDate result)

// From int YYYYMMDD
static NepaliDate NepaliDate.FromInt(int yyyymmdd)
```

### Properties

```csharp
int Year         // BS year (1901-2199)
int Month        // BS month (1-12)
int Day          // BS day (1-32)
DateTime EnglishDate    // Gregorian equivalent
DayOfWeek DayOfWeek     // .NET DayOfWeek enum
string DayName          // "Sunday", "Monday", ...
NepaliMonths MonthName  // Enum value for month
int MonthEndDay         // Last day of this month (29, 30, 31, or 32)
bool IsLeapYear         // Whether the BS year is a leap year
NepaliDate YearStartDate   // 1st of same BS year
NepaliDate YearEndDate     // Last day of same BS year
NepaliDate MonthStartDate  // 1st of same BS month
NepaliDate MonthEndDate    // Last day of same BS month

// Calendar metadata (2001-2089 BS; returns empty/default outside range without throwing)
string TithiNp       // Tithi (lunar day) in Nepali Devanagari; empty string outside 2001-2089 BS
string TithiEn       // Tithi transliterated to English; empty string outside 2001-2089 BS
bool IsPublicHoliday // true when gazetted public holiday; false outside 2001-2089 BS
string[] EventsNp    // Event names in Devanagari; empty array if none
string[] EventsEn    // Event names in English; empty array if none
CalendarInfo GetCalendarInfo() // All five fields in a single lookup; prefer this over per-property access in loops
```

### Arithmetic

```csharp
NepaliDate AddDays(double days)
NepaliDate AddMonths(double months, bool awayFromMonthEnd = false)
NepaliDate AddYears(int years, bool awayFromMonthEnd = false)
TimeSpan   operator -(NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right) // Total days between two dates

// Operators
bool operator ==(NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)
bool operator !=(NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)
bool operator < (NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)
bool operator > (NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)
bool operator <=(NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)
bool operator >=(NepaliDate left, NepaliDate right)

int CompareTo(NepaliDate other)
bool Equals(NepaliDate other)
```

### Fiscal Year properties

Nepal's fiscal year: Shrawan 1 (month 4) through Ashadh end (month 3 of next year).

```csharp
NepaliDate FiscalYearStartDate     // Shrawan 1 of the current fiscal year
NepaliDate FiscalYearEndDate       // Ashadh last-day of the current fiscal year
FiscalYearQuarters FiscalYearQuarter  // Current quarter
```

---

## Formatting

### ToString overloads

```csharp
// Default format: "2080-06-15"
string ToString()

// With DateFormats enum
string ToString(DateFormats format)

// With DateFormats and Separators
string ToString(DateFormats format, Separators separator)

// With Nepali Unicode digits
string ToString(DateFormats format, Separators separator, bool useNepaliDigits)

// IFormattable: format string is "<DateFormats>-<Separators>" e.g. "yMd-Slash"
string ToString(string? format, IFormatProvider? formatProvider)
```

### DateFormats enum

| Member | Example output |
|---|---|
| `yMd` | 2080-06-15 |
| `dMy` | 15-6-2080 |
| `mDy` | 6/15/2080 |
| `dMMMy` | 15 Ashwin 2080 |
| `ddMMMMyyyy` | 15 Aswin 2080 |
| `yMMMd` | 2080 Ashwin 15 |

### Separators enum

`Dash` (-), `Slash` (/), `Dot` (.), `Space` ( ), `None` (no separator), `Comma` (,)

---

## Smart Parsing

`NepaliDate.Parse` accepts:
- Numeric: `"2080-6-15"`, `"2080/6/15"`, `"2080.6.15"`, `"20800615"` (int-string)
- Nepali digits: `"२०८०-०६-१५"`
- Month names (100+ spellings): `"2080 Ashwin 15"`, `"2080 Aswin 15"`, `"2080 Asoj 15"`, `"Ashwin 15, 2080"`, etc.
- Mixed: `"15 Aswin, 2080"`, `"2080 Aswin 15th"`

Accepted month name variants (partial list):
- Baisakh: Baisakh, Baishakh, Vaishakh, Baisak, Besak
- Jestha: Jestha, Jeshtha, Jesth, Jeth
- Ashadh: Ashadh, Ashad, Asad, Asar, Ashar
- Shrawan: Shrawan, Shravan, Sawan, Saun
- Bhadra: Bhadra, Bhadau, Bhado, Bhadon, Bhaadra
- Ashwin: Ashwin, Aswin, Asoj, Aaswin, Ashoj
- Kartik: Kartik, Kartick, Kartika, Karthik
- Mangsir: Mangsir, Mangshir, Margashirsha, Aghan
- Poush: Poush, Push, Paush, Pous, Posh
- Magh: Magh, Maagh
- Falgun: Falgun, Phalgun, Phagun, Phalguna, Faagun
- Chaitra: Chaitra, Chait, Chatra

---

## NepaliDateRange

`namespace NepDate`
```csharp
struct NepaliDateRange : IEnumerable<NepaliDate>

// Constructor
NepaliDateRange(NepaliDate start, NepaliDate end)

// Properties
NepaliDate Start  { get; }
NepaliDate End    { get; }
int DayCount      { get; }  // Number of days in range (inclusive)

// Methods
bool Contains(NepaliDate date)
bool Overlaps(NepaliDateRange other)
bool IsContiguousWith(NepaliDateRange other)
NepaliDateRange Union(NepaliDateRange other)
NepaliDateRange Intersect(NepaliDateRange other)
NepaliDateRange Except(NepaliDateRange other)

IEnumerator<NepaliDate> GetEnumerator()
```

**Example:**
```csharp
var range = new NepaliDateRange(new NepaliDate(2080, 4, 1), new NepaliDate(2080, 6, 30));
foreach (var day in range)
    Console.WriteLine(day);

bool inQ1 = range.Contains(new NepaliDate(2080, 5, 15)); // true
```

---

## Bulk Conversion

`namespace NepDate`
`static class BulkConvert`

```csharp
// Convert many AD dates to BS (auto-parallelizes for large inputs)
static IEnumerable<NepaliDate> ToNepaliDates(IEnumerable<DateTime> englishDates)

// Convert many BS dates to AD (auto-parallelizes for large inputs)
static IEnumerable<DateTime> ToEnglishDates(IEnumerable<NepaliDate> nepaliDates)
```

Thread-safe. Results preserve input order.

---

## Calendar Data

For dates in 2001-2089 BS, access metadata via `date.GetCalendarInfo()` which returns a `CalendarInfo` struct:

```csharp
CalendarInfo info = date.GetCalendarInfo();

// info.TithiNp         - string:   tithi in Nepali Devanagari; empty string if none
// info.TithiEn         - string:   tithi transliterated to English; empty string if none
// info.IsPublicHoliday - bool:     true if Nepal gazetted public holiday
// info.EventsNp        - string[]: event/observance names in Devanagari; empty array if none
// info.EventsEn        - string[]: event/observance names in English; empty array if none
```

Returns empty/default values for dates outside 2001-2089 BS. No exception is thrown.

---

## Serialization

### System.Text.Json (automatic)

`NepaliDate` has `[JsonConverter(typeof(NepDateJsonConverter))]` applied, so no configuration needed:

```csharp
string json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(new { Date = new NepaliDate(2080, 6, 15) });
// Output: {"Date":"2080-06-15"}

var obj = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MyDto>(json);
// obj.Date is NepaliDate { Year=2080, Month=6, Day=15 }
```

### Newtonsoft.Json

```csharp
using NepDate.Serialization;

var settings = new JsonSerializerSettings();
settings.Converters.Add(new NepDateNewtonsoftConverter());
string json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(value, settings);
```

### XML / DataContract

`NepaliDate` implements `IXmlSerializable`. Serializes as `<Date>2080-06-15</Date>`.

```csharp
var serializer = new XmlSerializer(typeof(MyType));
using var writer = new StringWriter();
serializer.Serialize(writer, obj);
```

### ASP.NET Model Binding

`NepaliDate` has a registered `TypeConverter`, enabling automatic binding in ASP.NET MVC/Web API from route parameters, query strings, and form fields:

```csharp
[HttpGet("{date}")]
public IActionResult GetRecord(NepaliDate date) { ... }
// Works for: /api/records/2080-6-15
// Also works for: ?date=2080-6-15
```

---

## Type System

### Interfaces implemented

| Interface | Target | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IComparable<NepaliDate>` | both | Enables sorting and ordering |
| `IEquatable<NepaliDate>` | both | Enables == and .Equals() |
| `IFormattable` | both | Enables ToString(format, provider) |
| `ISpanFormattable` | net8.0 | Enables span-based formatting |
| `IParsable<NepaliDate>` | net8.0 | Enables NepaliDate.Parse(s, provider) |
| `IXmlSerializable` | both | Enables XML serialization |

### TypeConverter

`NepaliDateTypeConverter` is registered via `[TypeConverter]`. Converts:
- `string` -> `NepaliDate` (via Parse)
- `int` -> `NepaliDate` (YYYYMMDD format)
- `DateTime` -> `NepaliDate`
- `NepaliDate` -> any of the above

---

## Enums

### NepaliMonths

```csharp
enum NepaliMonths
{
    Baisakh  = 1,
    Jestha   = 2,
    Ashadh   = 3,
    Shrawan  = 4,
    Bhadra   = 5,
    Ashwin   = 6,
    Kartik   = 7,
    Mangsir  = 8,
    Poush    = 9,
    Magh     = 10,
    Falgun   = 11,
    Chaitra  = 12
}
```

### DateFormats

```csharp
enum DateFormats { yMd, dMy, mDy, dMMMy, ddMMMMyyyy, yMMMd }
```

### Separators

```csharp
enum Separators { Dash, Slash, Dot, Space, None, Comma }
```

### FiscalYearQuarters

```csharp
enum FiscalYearQuarters
{
    Current = 0,   // Resolved dynamically based on current month
    First   = 4,   // Shrawan-Bhadra (months 4-6)
    Second  = 7,   // Ashwin-Poush (months 7-9)
    Third   = 10,  // Magh-Chaitra (months 10-12)
    Fourth  = 1    // Baisakh-Ashadh (months 1-3)
}
```

---

## Exceptions

| Type | Namespace | Thrown when |
|---|---|---|
| `InvalidNepaliDateFormatException` | NepDate | Input string cannot be parsed as a valid BS date |
| `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` | System | Year, month, or day is numerically out of range |
| `NepaliDateOutOfRangeException` | NepDate | Date falls outside the supported 1901-2199 BS range |
| `NepaliMonthEndException` | NepDate | Day exceeds the month's maximum day count for that year |

---

## Advanced

### Struct design rationale

`NepaliDate` is a `readonly partial struct` - instances live on the stack, not the heap. This eliminates GC pressure. In a tight loop over thousands of dates (e.g., bulk calendar rendering, payroll processing) there are zero allocations from date creation or arithmetic.

### Thread safety

All instance methods and static methods are thread-safe. The struct is immutable by design. `BulkConvert` uses `Parallel.ForEach` internally and is safe for concurrent invocation.

### Internalized dependencies

Newtonsoft.Json and System.Text.Json support are compiled into the NepDate assembly using `PrivateAssets=all`. They do not appear as transitive dependencies in consuming projects.

### Supported range detail

| BS | AD (approximate) |
|---|---|
| 1901/01/01 BS | 1844-04-13 AD |
| 2199/12/30 BS | 2143-04-12 AD |

Calendar metadata: 2001-2089 BS. Outside this range: TithiNp/TithiEn return empty string, IsPublicHoliday returns false, EventsNp/EventsEn return empty arrays. No exception is thrown.

---
